input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (ItemsPage.xaml)**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <CollectionView.RefreshCommand>
            <Binding Path="RefreshCommand" />
        </CollectionView.RefreshCommand>

        <!-- Wrap in a RefreshView for pull-to-refresh -->
    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

Actually, `CollectionView` doesn't directly support pull-to-refresh — you need to wrap it in a **`RefreshView`**:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                 IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

        <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                        SelectionMode="Single"
                        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}"
                        SelectionChangedCommandParameter="{Binding SelectedItem, Source={RelativeSource Self}}">

            <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
                <DataTemplate>
                    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="12">
                        <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" FontSize="14" TextColor="Gray" />
                    </VerticalStackLayout>
                </DataTemplate>
            </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>

        </CollectionView>
    </RefreshView>
</ContentPage>
```

**ViewModel (ItemsViewModel.cs)**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api, INavigationService nav)
    {
        _api = api;
        _nav = nav;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelected(Item item)
    {
        if (item is null) return;

        // Shell navigation with the item as a query parameter
        await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
            nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = item });
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource → Items` | `ObservableCollection` drives the list |
| `IsRefreshing → IsRefreshing` | Two-way; set `false` when load completes to dismiss the spinner |
| `RefreshView.Command → RefreshCommand` | Generated by `[RelayCommand]` on `Refresh()` |
| `SelectionChangedCommand → ItemSelectedCommand` | Fires on tap; passes the tapped item via `SelectionChangedCommandParameter` |

**Important notes:**
- **`RefreshView` wraps `CollectionView`** — `CollectionView` has no built-in refresh support.
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` at the end of your refresh logic or the spinner never stops.
- Use **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** (`ObservableObject`, `[RelayCommand]`, `[ObservableProperty]`) to eliminate boilerplate.
- Register the route in `AppShell.xaml.cs`: `Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(DetailPage), typeof(DetailPage));`
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI CollectionView with single selection and pull-to-refresh, plus navigation to a detail page. This is a general knowledge question about MAUI/XAML development - no codebase exploration needed. I'll provide the XAML and ViewModel explanation directly.
